title: Management of Gunshot Wound to face
author: Hameed, Faisal
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Gunshot injuries to the face involve a combination of high‑energy tissue damage, contamination, and immediate threats to airway and bleeding. Management begins with securing the airway, as distorted anatomy, bleeding, and edema can rapidly obstruct it. Hemorrhage control follows through packing, pressure, or vessel ligation. The wound is assessed for trajectory, bone involvement, soft‑tissue loss, and associated ocular or intracranial injuries. Early irrigation and debridement reduce infection risk, while reconstruction focuses on restoring the bony framework first, then soft‑tissue coverage using local, regional, or free flaps. Throughout care, functional outcomes—occlusion, speech, swallowing, and eyelid competence—guide decision‑making, supported by a multidisciplinary team.
